scope: The Stoic philosophical tradition from its Hellenistic founding through its Roman flowering to its modern revival

papers.title: Lives of Eminent Philosophers, Book VII, Chapter 1: Zeno
papers.venue: Loeb Classical Library ed., trans. R.D. Hicks (Perseus Digital Library)
papers.inclusion_reason: The single most extensive ancient biographical and doxographical source on Zeno of Citium -- covers his life, his break from the Academy/Cynics, and a systematic summary of Old Stoic logic, physics, and ethics as his generation formulated it.

papers.title: Lives of Eminent Philosophers, Book VII, Chapter 7: Chrysippus
papers.venue: Loeb Classical Library ed., trans. R.D. Hicks (Perseus Digital Library)
papers.inclusion_reason: The main ancient account of the Stoa's systematizer, whose prolific (now almost entirely lost) output on logic, fate, and ethics reshaped the school -- includes the doxographical catalogue that anchors most modern reconstructions of his logic.

papers.title: De Finibus Bonorum et Malorum, Book III (Cato's exposition of Stoic ethics)
papers.venue: Loeb Classical Library ed., trans. H. Rackham, 1914 (Internet Archive)
papers.inclusion_reason: The only sustained, continuous ancient exposition of Old Stoic ethical theory (oikeiosis, the telos, virtue as the sole good) that survives outside doxographical summary -- delivered in the persona of the Stoic Cato and drawing directly on Chrysippus.

papers.title: De Natura Deorum, Book II (Balbus's exposition of Stoic theology)
papers.venue: Loeb Classical Library ed., trans. H. Rackham, 1933 (Internet Archive)
papers.inclusion_reason: The only connected ancient exposition of Stoic physics/theology to survive -- providence, the cosmos as a rational living being, and the argument from design as the Old Stoa formulated them, reported through the Stoic spokesman Balbus.

papers.title: On Stoic Self-Contradictions (De Stoicorum Repugnantiis), in Moralia XIII.2
papers.venue: Loeb Classical Library, vol. XIII part 2 (LCL 470), trans. Harold Cherniss, 1976 (Internet Archive)
papers.inclusion_reason: A hostile but detail-rich Middle Platonist polemic that quotes Chrysippus extensively verbatim -- one of the largest surviving caches of direct Chrysippean fragments on ethics, fate, and psychology, precisely because Plutarch is trying to catch him out.

papers.title: Stoicorum Veterum Fragmenta (Fragments of the Old Stoics)
papers.inclusion_reason: The foundational critical edition collecting the surviving Greek and Latin fragments of Zeno, Cleanthes, and Chrysippus in one place, organized by topic (logic, physics, ethics) -- still the standard reference ("SVF" citations) underlying virtually all modern scholarship on the Old Stoa.

papers.title: The Hellenistic Philosophers, Vol. 1: Translations of the Principal Sources, with Philosophical Commentary
papers.inclusion_reason: The standard modern sourcebook translating and thematically organizing the scattered testimonia on Old Stoic logic, physics, and ethics, with commentary that remains the default entry point for the field.

papers.title: Determinism and Freedom in Stoic Philosophy
papers.inclusion_reason: The definitive modern reconstruction of Chrysippus's theory of fate, causal determinism, and moral responsibility from the scattered fragments -- the standard scholarly treatment of the Old Stoa's most contested doctrine.

papers.title: Ethics and Human Action in Early Stoicism
papers.inclusion_reason: A detailed modern reconstruction of the Old Stoic psychology of action (impulse, assent, the passions) from Zeno through Chrysippus, foundational to how the founding generation's moral psychology is understood today.

papers.title: The Stoic Idea of the City
papers.inclusion_reason: The only systematic modern study of Zeno's lost Republic, reconstructing its radical political and cosmopolitan doctrine from scattered ancient citation -- essential for the founding generation's political philosophy, which is otherwise almost invisible in the corpus.

papers.title: Stoicism
papers.authors: Marion Durand, Simon Shogry, and Dirk Baltzly
papers.inclusion_reason: The current, editorially reviewed reference-work overview of the whole Stoic system (logic, physics, ethics) as the Old Stoa formulated its interlocking structure -- the natural orientation point for readers before the primary testimonia.

papers.title: Ancient Logic (see esp. §4, "The Stoics")
papers.inclusion_reason: A scholarly overview of Chrysippus's propositional logic (the indemonstrables, the analysis of arguments) by a leading specialist, giving modern readers a rigorous entry point into the Old Stoa's most technically demanding subfield.

papers.title: How to Be a Stoic: Using Ancient Philosophy to Live a Modern Life
papers.inclusion_reason: Flagship trade book of the contemporary Modern Stoicism revival by a credentialed academic philosopher (City College of New York); frames the movement's core pitch of Stoicism as a practical, secular philosophy of life.

papers.title: Toward the Fifth Stoa: The Return of Virtue Ethics
papers.inclusion_reason: Peer-reviewed academic article by a leading Modern Stoicism proponent arguing for Stoicism's place in the contemporary virtue-ethics revival; grounds the popular revival in a citable scholarly argument.

papers.title: A New Stoicism (Revised Edition)
papers.inclusion_reason: The central academic-philosophy reconstruction of Stoic ethics for contemporary metaethics, by a professor emeritus of philosophy (William & Mary) and former associate editor of Ethics; anchors the 'analytic Neostoicism' strand of the revival distinct from the popular self-help strand.

papers.title: Stoicisms Ancient and Modern
papers.inclusion_reason: Essay by one of the most senior living Stoic scholars (Chancellor's Professor Emeritus of Classics, UC Berkeley) published directly on the Modern Stoicism organization's own site, assessing what the revival keeps and drops from ancient doctrine.

papers.title: Report on Stoic Week 2021
papers.inclusion_reason: The Modern Stoicism organization's own published research report on its flagship Stoic Week program, the primary empirical documentation of the revival's central public-engagement intervention and its claimed well-being effects.

papers.title: Being Better: Stoicism for a World Worth Living In
papers.inclusion_reason: A recent, distinct strand of the revival applying Stoicism to social justice, climate, and civic life, by two credentialed academics (Whiting: lecturer/researcher in Stoicism and sustainability, UCLouvain; Konstantakos: PhD, philosophy and classics instructor); shows the revival is not monolithically self-help.

papers.title: Against the Stoics on Common Conceptions (De Communibus Notitiis)
papers.venue: Moralia, Loeb Classical Library 470 (Harvard University Press)
papers.inclusion_reason: Companion ancient polemic to 'On Stoic Self-Contradictions,' arguing Stoic doctrine conflicts with humanity's own shared intuitions ('common conceptions'); together the two essays are the canonical Academic/Peripatetic case against Stoicism from antiquity.

papers.title: Phenomenology of Spirit (Self-Consciousness: 'Freedom of Self-Consciousness: Stoicism, Scepticism, and the Unhappy Consciousness')
papers.authors: G. W. F. Hegel (trans. A. V. Miller)
papers.inclusion_reason: The canonical modern philosophical critique of Stoicism prior to the 20th century: Hegel casts Stoic freedom as a merely formal, abstract withdrawal into thought that fails to engage the world, arising as 'a slave's response' -- a primary text that any serious treatment of philosophical objections to Stoicism must engage.

papers.title: The Therapy of Desire: Theory and Practice in Hellenistic Ethics
papers.inclusion_reason: Major philosophical critique arguing the Stoic recommendation to extirpate the passions rests on an impoverished, undifferentiated view of the psyche and fails to offer a fully satisfying account of emotional life -- a direct academic source for the 'apatheia as inhuman' objection.

papers.title: Misunderstood Stoicism: The Negative Association Between Stoic Ideology and Well-Being
papers.authors: Johannes Alfons Karl, Paul Verhaeghen, Shelley N. Aikman, Stian Solem, Espen R. Lassen, Ronald Fischer
papers.inclusion_reason: Peer-reviewed primary empirical study across three countries finding popular ('naive') Stoic ideology -- especially taciturnity and suppression of emotion -- is negatively associated with well-being, a direct modern empirical challenge to the revival movement's central wellness claims.

papers.title: Three Reasons Not to Be a Stoic (But Try Nietzsche Instead)
papers.inclusion_reason: Essay by a credentialed academic (Adjunct Fellow, Macquarie University) in an editorially reviewed outlet, giving a compact, citable statement of contemporary objections to Stoicism (passivity/quietism, emotional suppression, the internal/external double standard).

papers.title: De Constantia (Two Bookes of Constancie), trans. Sir John Stradling (1595)
papers.venue: Internet Archive (Stradling trans., ed. Rudolf Kirk, 1939)
papers.inclusion_reason: The founding text of Neostoicism itself -- Lipsius's own Stoic dialogue of consolation, the primary document any account of the movement's origin must cite.

papers.title: Politica: Six Books of Politics or Political Instruction, ed. and trans. Jan Waszink (2004)
papers.venue: Princeton University Library catalog (Royal Van Gorcum bilingual critical edition)
papers.inclusion_reason: Lipsius's second major Neostoic work, applying Stoic constancy to statecraft; the text Christopher Brooke's Philosophic Pride uses as its starting point for tracing Stoicism's influence on political thought.

papers.title: The Morall Philosophy of the Stoicks (La Philosophie morale des Stoïques), trans. Charles Cotton (1664)
papers.venue: Internet Archive (Early English Books Online digitization of the 1667 Mortlock imprint)
papers.inclusion_reason: The second founding Neostoic primary text alongside Lipsius's De Constantia -- du Vair's own rendering of Epictetus into a Christianized Stoic ethics, independently influential on the French moraliste tradition.

papers.title: Justus Lipsius
papers.inclusion_reason: Editorially authoritative reference-work overview of Lipsius's life, De Constantia, and Politica, and the philological method by which he re-founded Stoicism for a Christian readership.

papers.title: Neostoicism and the Early Modern State
papers.authors: Gerhard Oestreich (trans. David McLintock, ed. Brigitta Oestreich and H. G. Koenigsberger)
papers.venue: Cambridge University Press (Cambridge Studies in Early Modern History)
papers.inclusion_reason: The classic historical study of how Neostoicism shaped early modern statecraft, military discipline, and administration -- the standard reference the whole historiography of Neostoicism responds to.

papers.title: Philosophic Pride: Stoicism and Political Thought from Lipsius to Rousseau
papers.inclusion_reason: The essential full-length treatment of Stoicism's transmission through early modern political philosophy from Lipsius through Grotius, Hobbes, and eventually Rousseau -- the modern scholarly anchor for this whole subarea.

papers.title: Stoics and Neostoics: Rubens and the Circle of Lipsius
papers.inclusion_reason: The fullest study of Lipsius's intellectual circle and how his Neostoicism radiated into humanist and artistic culture (via Rubens), grounding the movement's reception beyond philosophy narrowly construed.

papers.title: Spinoza and the Stoics
papers.inclusion_reason: The first systematic scholarly treatment of the parallels between Spinoza's metaphysics, psychology, and ethics and their Stoic counterparts -- documents Stoicism's specific influence on early modern rationalism.

papers.title: Reading Descartes as a Stoic: Appropriate Action, Virtue, and the Passions
papers.inclusion_reason: Peer-reviewed article directly tracing Stoic ethical concepts (appropriate action, virtue, the passions) into Descartes's moral philosophy -- the specific Descartes-Stoicism engagement this subarea's brief calls for.

papers.title: The Early Modern Legacy of the Stoics
papers.venue: in J. Klein and N. Powers (eds.), The Oxford Handbook of Hellenistic Philosophy, Oxford University Press, pp. 661-680
papers.inclusion_reason: Recent synthesizing chapter by a leading specialist on Stoicism's reception, surveying its early modern afterlife from Lipsius and du Vair through Descartes and Spinoza -- ties the whole subarea together.

papers.title: Justus Lipsius's De Constantia: A Stoic Spiritual Exercise
papers.venue: Poetics Today, vol. 28, no. 3, pp. 339-362 (Duke University Press)
papers.inclusion_reason: Peer-reviewed close reading of De Constantia as a Stoic spiritual exercise in the Hadot sense, connecting the ancient practice-based reading of Stoicism (a likely theme elsewhere in this survey) to its Renaissance revival.

papers.title: Moral Letters to Lucilius (Epistulae Morales ad Lucilium)
papers.venue: Loeb Classical Library ed., trans. Richard M. Gummere (Wikisource)
papers.inclusion_reason: Seneca's 124-letter collection is the single most-cited primary text of Roman Stoic ethics and epistolary philosophy; foundational to the entire subarea.

papers.title: De Ira (On Anger)
papers.venue: Bohn's Classical Library ed., trans. Aubrey Stewart (Wikisource)
papers.inclusion_reason: Seneca's fullest treatment of a single passion and its Stoic therapy; a distinct, separately citable three-book primary work.

papers.title: De Providentia (On Providence)
papers.venue: Bohn's Classical Library ed., trans. Aubrey Stewart (Wikisource)
papers.inclusion_reason: Seneca's primary-text answer to the theodicy problem within Stoic providence and fate -- a distinct dialogue in its own right.

papers.title: De Vita Beata (On the Happy Life)
papers.venue: Bohn's Classical Library ed., trans. Aubrey Stewart (Wikisource)
papers.inclusion_reason: Seneca's direct treatment of eudaimonia and virtue as the sole good, a core primary text for Stoic ethical theory.

papers.title: De Tranquillitate Animi (On Tranquility of Mind)
papers.venue: Bohn's Classical Library ed., trans. Aubrey Stewart (Wikisource)
papers.inclusion_reason: A distinct dialogue on the Stoic practice of achieving equanimity amid worldly business, addressed to Serenus.

papers.title: De Constantia Sapientis (On the Firmness of the Wise Person)
papers.venue: Bohn's Classical Library ed., trans. Aubrey Stewart (Wikisource)
papers.inclusion_reason: Seneca's primary-text defense of the Stoic sage's imperturbability against injury and insult, a distinct dialogue.

papers.title: De Otio (On Leisure)
papers.inclusion_reason: Seneca's distinct treatment of withdrawal from public life and the Stoic duty to the wider cosmopolis, a separately citable (if fragmentary) dialogue.

papers.title: De Clementia (On Mercy)
papers.inclusion_reason: Seneca's applied treatise addressed to Nero on Stoic political ethics and the virtue of clemency in the ruler -- a distinct work from the personal-ethics dialogues.

papers.title: Naturales Quaestiones (Natural Questions)
papers.inclusion_reason: Seneca's distinct primary work on Stoic natural philosophy/physics, complementing the ethical dialogues and letters with the tradition's cosmology.

papers.title: Discourses
papers.inclusion_reason: The primary surviving record of Epictetus's oral teaching, foundational to Roman Stoic practical ethics and later a direct influence on Marcus Aurelius.

papers.title: Enchiridion (Handbook)
papers.venue: trans. Elizabeth Carter (The Internet Classics Archive, MIT)
papers.inclusion_reason: Arrian's condensed handbook distilling Epictetus's Discourses into a separately transmitted, independently canonical primary text.

papers.title: Meditations
papers.inclusion_reason: The private philosophical notebook of the last of the Five Good Emperors, the terminal and most widely read primary text of the Roman Stoa.

papers.title: Stoic Ethics
papers.inclusion_reason: Peer-reviewed reference-work entry devoted specifically to Stoic ethical theory (virtue, the good, indifferents, the sage), complementing the broader SEP entry with a narrower focus.

papers.title: The Morality of Happiness
papers.inclusion_reason: The standard comprehensive scholarly treatment of ancient eudaimonist ethics; its chapters on Stoic virtue, self-concern, and the role of nature are foundational secondary literature for this subarea.

papers.title: The Stoic Life: Emotions, Duties, and Fate
papers.inclusion_reason: A rigorous, textually grounded monograph on Stoic ethics that directly treats the passions/eupatheiai, indifferents, and duty (kathekon); a core secondary source for the subarea.

papers.title: Stoic Studies
papers.venue: Cambridge University Press (reissued University of California Press)
papers.inclusion_reason: Collected essays by a leading Hellenistic-philosophy scholar, including "The Harmonics of Stoic Virtue" and other pieces central to the modern scholarly understanding of Stoic virtue and psychology.

papers.title: Knowledge, Nature, and the Good: Essays on Ancient Philosophy
papers.inclusion_reason: Includes several essays central to Stoic ethics and virtue theory (e.g. "Stoic Autonomy" and studies of Stoic moral improvement in Seneca and Marcus Aurelius) by a major figure in ancient philosophy scholarship.

papers.title: Stoicism and Emotion
papers.inclusion_reason: The definitive modern scholarly treatment of the Stoic theory of passions and eupatheiai ("good emotions"), directly on-topic for this subarea's core doctrines.

papers.title: The Stoic Sage: The Early Stoics on Wisdom, Sagehood and Socrates
papers.inclusion_reason: The first book-length scholarly study of the Stoic ideal of the sage, tracing the concept of wisdom/sagehood from Socrates through the early Stoa with close attention to the primary evidence.

papers.title: Following Nature: A Study in Stoic Ethics
papers.venue: Oxford Studies in Ancient Philosophy, vol. 9
papers.inclusion_reason: A canonical, widely cited peer-reviewed study connecting the Stoic ethical end ("living in agreement with nature") to oikeiosis and virtue -- core to how the subarea's doctrines fit together.

papers.title: Psychological Disease and Action-Guiding Impressions in Early Stoicism
papers.venue: British Journal for the History of Philosophy, vol. 29, no. 5
papers.inclusion_reason: Recent peer-reviewed scholarship on Stoic moral psychology -- how vice and passion distort action-guiding impressions -- extending the subarea's coverage of the passions into current research.

papers.title: Being Virtuous and Doing the Right Thing
papers.venue: Proceedings and Addresses of the American Philosophical Association, vol. 78, no. 2
papers.inclusion_reason: Presidential address by a leading ancient-ethics scholar defending virtue-centered ethical theory (of which Stoicism is a paradigm case) against the objection that it cannot guide action.

papers.title: Lives of Eminent Philosophers, Book VII (Zeno and the Stoics)
papers.inclusion_reason: Primary ancient doxography for Stoic logic (the Stoic dialecticians, propositional forms, the theory of lekta) and physics/cosmology; the fullest surviving connected account of the school's system, especially Chrysippus.

papers.title: De Fato (On Fate)
papers.inclusion_reason: Primary ancient source transmitting Chrysippus's arguments on causal determinism, the Lazy Argument, and co-fated events -- central evidence for Stoic logical/physical fatalism and how it was defended against critics.

papers.title: The Stoics on Determinism and Compatibilism
papers.inclusion_reason: Reconstructs the Stoic arguments for universal necessitation and distinguishes four compatibilist strategies reconciling determinism with moral responsibility, in explicit dialogue with the modern debate (including Frankfurt).

papers.title: Stoic Logic
papers.inclusion_reason: The classic twentieth-century study that first demonstrated Stoic logic as a genuine, formally rigorous propositional system distinct from and comparable to Aristotelian syllogistic; still the starting reference for the subject.

papers.title: Stoic Natural Philosophy (Physics and Cosmology)
papers.venue: The Cambridge Companion to the Stoics (Cambridge University Press)
papers.inclusion_reason: Focused survey chapter on Stoic physics and cosmology -- the active/passive principles, pneuma, and cosmic cycles -- by a specialist in ancient natural philosophy, in the standard companion volume edited by Brad Inwood.

papers.title: Stoic Theology
papers.venue: The Cambridge Companion to the Stoics (Cambridge University Press)
papers.inclusion_reason: Companion chapter to White's physics chapter, covering the providential rational cosmos, fate, and divination -- the point at which Stoic physics underwrites theology and, in turn, the ethics.

papers.title: Hellenistic Physics and Metaphysics (Stoic physics and metaphysics)
papers.venue: The Cambridge History of Hellenistic Philosophy (Cambridge University Press)
papers.inclusion_reason: Wide-ranging scholarly analysis of Stoic physics as a materialist continuum theory, systematically contrasted with Epicurean atomist physics, in the field's major reference history of the period.

papers.title: Determinism and Indeterminism
papers.venue: The Cambridge History of Hellenistic Philosophy (Cambridge University Press)
papers.inclusion_reason: Covers logic and contingency, the Stoic response to the Master Argument, the Chrysippean notion of fate, and fate's relation to responsibility and divination -- directly bridges the logic and physics of Stoic determinism.

papers.title: Physics of the Stoics
papers.venue: Routledge & Kegan Paul (reissued Princeton University Press)
papers.inclusion_reason: Landmark history-of-science monograph reconstructing Stoic physics as a continuum theory (pneuma, tension, cohesion), still cited as a starting point for the physics of the Stoa and its influence.

papers.title: Enchiridion (The Handbook)
papers.inclusion_reason: The single most compressed statement of Stoic praxis: the dichotomy of control opens the text and the whole work is organized as a training manual (askesis), not a treatise -- the primary anchor for this subarea.

papers.title: Lectures and Fragments
papers.authors: Musonius Rufus (trans. Cora E. Lutz, as "Musonius Rufus: The Roman Socrates")
papers.venue: Yale Classical Studies 10 (1947), pp. 3-147 -- Internet Archive
papers.inclusion_reason: Roman Stoicism's most explicit primary voice on philosophy as habituated practice (askesis) over verbal instruction -- his lectures argue directly that training, not theory, is what makes one good, a load-bearing primary text for this subarea's thesis.

papers.title: The Inner Citadel: The Meditations of Marcus Aurelius
papers.inclusion_reason: Hadot's book-length demonstration of the three-discipline (desire/assent/action) reading of Marcus Aurelius -- the single most detailed application of the "way of life" method to a Stoic text, foundational to this subarea alongside his 1995 book.

papers.title: What Is Ancient Philosophy?
papers.inclusion_reason: Hadot's broadest synthesis, situating Stoic askesis within the wider ancient practice of philosophy as a chosen way of life across schools -- extends and contextualizes the 1995/1998 books' claims about Stoic practice specifically.

papers.title: Epictetus: A Stoic and Socratic Guide to Life
papers.inclusion_reason: The leading scholarly monograph on Epictetus specifically, with fresh translations and sustained analysis of his training program for volition (prohairesis) and correct use of impressions -- direct, authoritative treatment of this subarea's central primary figure.

papers.title: Stoic Ethics: Epictetus and Happiness as Freedom
papers.inclusion_reason: A focused specialist study of how Epictetus's practical program (control over judgment, desire, and action) is meant to produce freedom from fear and dependence on luck -- deepens the subarea's coverage of Epictetus's practice-oriented ethics beyond Long's more general treatment.

papers.title: Learning to Live Naturally: Stoic Ethics and its Modern Significance
papers.inclusion_reason: Recent authoritative scholarship whose second part is specifically on Stoic ethical development -- "learning to live naturally" as a practiced, progressive training -- bringing the subarea's coverage up to current scholarly consensus and connecting practice to Stoic developmental psychology.

papers.title: Epictetus
papers.venue: Stanford Encyclopedia of Philosophy (first published 2008, substantive revision 2025)
papers.inclusion_reason: Editorially reviewed reference synthesis of Epictetus's training program -- volition, the correct use of impressions, and the three topoi of desire/impulse/assent -- with an account of recent scholarly debate over how tightly systematic that framework really is (relevant nuance against over-reading the "three disciplines" as tidy doctrine).

papers.title: A New Guide to Rational Living
papers.authors: Albert Ellis and Robert A. Harper
papers.venue: Wilshire Book Co. (revised ed. 1975; orig. published 1961 as A Guide to Rational Living)
papers.inclusion_reason: Ellis's popular-clinical restatement of REBT for lay and clinical readers, explicit about its Stoic lineage; the book that carried Epictetus's dictum into mainstream psychotherapy practice.

papers.title: Cognitive Therapy and the Emotional Disorders
papers.inclusion_reason: Beck's foundational text establishing cognitive therapy, opening with Epictetus's maxim on judgment and disturbance and explicitly tracing the theory's philosophical origins to the Stoics.

papers.title: Cognitive Therapy of Depression
papers.authors: Aaron T. Beck, A. John Rush, Brian F. Shaw, and Gary Emery
papers.inclusion_reason: The canonical CBT treatment manual built directly on Beck's Stoic-derived cognitive model; still the field's primary reference for how the theory cashes out clinically.

papers.title: The Philosophy of Cognitive-Behavioural Therapy (CBT): Stoic Philosophy as Rational and Cognitive Psychotherapy
papers.inclusion_reason: The most thorough academic monograph on the Stoicism-CBT relationship, from an established publisher, tracing both theoretical parallels and practical technique transfer from Stoic philosophy into CBT.

papers.title: Stoic Philosophy as a Cognitive-Behavioral Therapy
papers.authors: Donald Robertson and R. Trent Codd III
papers.venue: The Behavior Therapist, 42(2) (Association for Behavioral and Cognitive Therapies)
papers.inclusion_reason: Peer-reviewed piece in ABCT's own house journal arguing Stoicism functioned as a genuine ancient system of psychotherapy, written by a clinician co-author -- authoritative professional-body engagement with the lineage claim.

papers.title: Back to the Future: Stoic Wisdom and Psychotherapy for Neuropsychiatric Conditions
papers.inclusion_reason: Peer-reviewed clinical-academic review applying Stoic cognitive technique to neuropsychiatric treatment, extending the Stoicism-CBT lineage claim into a specific clinical subfield.

papers.title: Stoic Cognitive Theories and Contemporary Neuropsychological Treatments
papers.inclusion_reason: Peer-reviewed philosophy-journal article examining Stoic cognitive-distancing and present-moment attention techniques as forerunners of contemporary neuropsychological treatment tools.

papers.title: The Development and Validation of the Stoic Attitudes and Behaviours Scale
papers.authors: Tim LeBon, Gary Brown, Raymond DiGiuseppe, Johannes Karl, Ronald Fischer, and Gregory Lopez
papers.inclusion_reason: Primary peer-reviewed empirical psychology study validating a psychometric scale for measuring Stoic attitudes/behaviours, distinguishing seven dimensions -- direct empirical evidence for Stoicism as a measurable psychological construct.

papers.title: A Randomized, Controlled, Preliminary Study to Assess the Efficacy of Logic-Based Therapy in Reducing Anxiety and/or Depression in Family Caregivers
papers.authors: Elliot D. Cohen, Barbara Piozzini, Chinmay Bapat, Jiuqing Cheng, Pablo Tagore Palma Soza, Vishalha N. Punjani, and Himani Chaukar
papers.inclusion_reason: Primary peer-reviewed empirical study -- a randomized controlled trial directly testing a Stoic-derived psychotherapy (Logic-Based Therapy) against a control, providing clinical-efficacy evidence rather than historical/theoretical argument alone.

papers.title: Lives of Eminent Philosophers, Book VII, Chapter 4: Cleanthes
papers.venue: Loeb Classical Library ed., trans. R.D. Hicks (Perseus Digital Library)
papers.inclusion_reason: The primary ancient testimony on Zeno's successor as head of the Stoa, whose Hymn to Zeus and physical doctrine (esp. on the soul and the cosmos as a living being) are otherwise lost.

papers.title: Against the Logicians (Adversus Mathematicos VII-VIII)
papers.venue: Loeb Classical Library, vol. II (LCL 291), trans. R.G. Bury, 1935 (Internet Archive)
papers.inclusion_reason: The fullest surviving ancient treatment of Stoic epistemology and propositional logic (the kataleptic impression, the criterion of truth, the analysis of the sign) as Chrysippus's school articulated it, reported (skeptically) by a near-contemporary of the tradition it describes.

papers.title: Hellenistic Philosophy: Stoics, Epicureans, Sceptics (2nd ed.)
papers.inclusion_reason: A widely used, broadly scoped scholarly introduction with substantial chapters devoted specifically to Zeno's, Cleanthes's, and Chrysippus's contributions to logic, physics, and ethics, situating the Old Stoa against its Academic and Peripatetic rivals.

papers.title: Making Sense of Stoic Indifferents
papers.venue: Oxford Studies in Ancient Philosophy, Volume 49, pp. 227-282
papers.inclusion_reason: Peer-reviewed treatment of the coherence problem this search angle names directly -- whether the preferred/dispreferred-indifferents doctrine can be squared with virtue being the sole good, including Aristo's ancient challenge and the Chrysippean response.

papers.title: Emotion and Peace of Mind: From Stoic Agitation to Christian Temptation
papers.inclusion_reason: Gifford Lectures-based study arguing we need not share the Stoic ideal of freedom from all emotion, only from unwanted ones, and identifying specific limits of the Stoic therapeutic method (e.g. against free-floating mood disorders) -- another primary academic source for the apatheia critique.

papers.title: Juste Lipse et la restauration du stoïcisme: étude et traduction des traités stoïciens
papers.venue: Librairie Philosophique J. Vrin (Philologie et Mercure)
papers.inclusion_reason: Major francophone scholarly study and translation of Lipsius's Stoic treatises, standard citation in the Neostoicism historiography and specifically named in this subarea's brief.

papers.title: Reason and Emotion in Psychotherapy
papers.inclusion_reason: Ellis's own primary statement founding REBT, in which he directly credits the ancient Stoics -- quoting Epictetus's Enchiridion -- as the source of REBT's central principle that people are disturbed not by events but by their views of them.

papers.title: Exploring the Philosophical Underpinnings of Cognitive Behavioral Therapy: Stoicism as a Guiding Philosophy
papers.inclusion_reason: Peer-reviewed academic-journal treatment specifically tracing CBT's theoretical foundations to Stoic philosophy, useful as a synthesis piece complementing the earlier Montgomery and Robertson scholarship.

papers.title: A Guide to the Good Life: The Ancient Art of Stoic Joy
papers.inclusion_reason: One of the two founding texts of the contemporary popular Stoic revival (alongside Pigliucci), by a philosophy professor (Wright State University), published by an academic press; introduced 'negative visualization' and the dichotomy of control to a mass audience.

papers.title: Theory and Practice of Logic-Based Therapy: Integrating Critical Thinking and Philosophy into Psychotherapy
papers.inclusion_reason: Academic monograph founding Logic-Based Therapy, a direct philosophical-counseling descendant of REBT that returns explicitly to Stoic and Socratic method -- the clearest case of Stoic technique institutionalized as a distinct clinical modality.

papers.title: Stoics as activists (subtitle: When Stoicism is a political not just a personal virtue)
papers.inclusion_reason: Essay by a credentialed philosopher (Associate Professor, Deakin University) in a reputable, editorially sourced long-form outlet, engaging the recurring modern charge (traced to Hegel) that Stoicism is self-involved and politically quietist.

papers.title: The Structured Self in Hellenistic and Roman Thought
papers.inclusion_reason: Major scholarly account of Stoic (and Epicurean) selfhood, the passions, and ethical character formation, grounded in close textual analysis by a leading ancient-philosophy scholar.

papers.title: Epitome of Stoic Ethics (excerpted in Stobaeus's Eclogae)
papers.authors: Arius Didymus (ed. and trans. Arthur J. Pomeroy)
papers.venue: Society of Biblical Literature, Texts and Translations 44 / Graeco-Roman Religion 14, ed. and trans. Arthur J. Pomeroy, 1999
papers.inclusion_reason: The most systematic surviving doxographical summary of Old Stoic ethics -- a structured epitome (transmitted via Stobaeus) independent of Diogenes Laertius, indispensable for reconstructing the school's own technical taxonomy of goods, values, and appropriate action.

papers.title: Stoicism in the Renaissance from Petrarch to Lipsius
papers.inclusion_reason: Authoritative survey by a leading historian of Renaissance philosophy tracing the two-century transmission of Stoic ideas from Petrarch through to Lipsius, the exact long-transmission arc this subarea covers.

papers.title: On Fate (De Fato)
papers.authors: Alexander of Aphrodisias (trans. and comm. R. W. Sharples)
papers.inclusion_reason: The most sustained ancient critique of Stoic determinism, written from within the rival Peripatetic tradition; modern scholars use it as primary evidence for reconstructing the Stoic positions it attacks, including the Master Argument's aftermath.

papers.title: Philosophy as a Way of Life: Spiritual Exercises from Socrates to Foucault
papers.authors: Pierre Hadot (ed. Arnold I. Davidson, trans. Michael Chase)
papers.inclusion_reason: The founding text of the entire interpretive tradition this subarea covers: Hadot coined and theorized "spiritual exercises" (exercices spirituels) as the category for reading ancient philosophy as a practiced way of life rather than doctrine -- Tier 1 as the originating work of this specific scholarly lens.

papers.title: The Ancient Origins of Cognitive Therapy: The Reemergence of Stoicism
papers.inclusion_reason: Early peer-reviewed academic paper systematically comparing Stoic concepts (Epictetus, Marcus Aurelius, Seneca, Chrysippus) against Beck's and Ellis's therapeutic frameworks -- one of the first scholarly treatments of the lineage.

papers.title: The Art of Living: The Stoics on the Nature and Function of Philosophy
papers.venue: Ashgate (2nd ed. Bristol Classical Press / Bloomsbury, 2009)
papers.inclusion_reason: A dedicated scholarly monograph on the Stoic conception of philosophy itself as a craft-like practical activity (technē peri ton bion) -- extends the Socratic-to-Stoic "art of living" thesis with close readings of Epictetus and Marcus Aurelius.

papers.title: The Western Origins of Mindfulness Therapy in Ancient Rome
papers.authors: Andrea E. Cavanna, Giulia Purpura, Anna Riva, Renata Nacinovich, and Stefano Seri
papers.inclusion_reason: Peer-reviewed article arguing Roman Stoic attention practices are direct antecedents of both CBT and mindfulness-based interventions, with explicit discussion of Ellis's and Beck's acknowledged debts to Epictetus.
